name: typer
summary: Build great CLIs with Python type hints — built on Click

story.problem: Click is powerful but requires you to duplicate information: write type annotations for your function, then add @click.option decorators with the same type information again. Typer eliminates that duplication by reading Python type hints directly.
story.mental_model: Typer is Click without the decorators. Annotate a function with Python types and Typer infers the CLI arguments automatically: str → text argument, int → integer option, bool → flag, Optional[str] → optional. The function signature is the CLI interface.
story.when_to_use: New Python CLI tools where you want minimal boilerplate. If your team knows Python type hints, typer CLIs are instantly readable. Works especially well with Pydantic models and FastAPI (same author, same philosophy).
story.when_not_to_use: If you're maintaining an existing Click-based CLI — just use Click directly. For very complex CLIs with custom Click types or plugins, the Click layer gives more control.

alternatives.name: click
alternatives.reason: More explicit, larger ecosystem, better for complex custom CLI behaviour

alternatives.name: argparse
alternatives.reason: Standard library, zero dependencies, sufficient for simple scripts

alternatives.name: fire
alternatives.reason: Even less boilerplate — turns any function/class into a CLI automatically

nodes.label: Typer (app)
nodes.summary: The app instance. Add commands to it and call app() as entry point.

nodes.label: @app.command()
nodes.summary: Register a function as a CLI command. Type hints define the arguments and options.

nodes.label: Argument()
nodes.summary: Annotate a positional argument with metadata: help text, default, validation.

nodes.label: Option()
nodes.summary: Annotate a named option (--flag) with metadata: help, prompt, envvar.

nodes.label: typer.echo()
nodes.summary: Print output. Wraps click.echo — prefers this over print() for CLI output.

nodes.label: typer.secho()
nodes.summary: Print with ANSI colour/style. Short for styled echo.

nodes.label: typer.Exit()
nodes.summary: Raise to exit with a specific return code without an error message.

steps.label: Write app
Run
steps.code:
python greet.py Alice
# Hello Alice

python greet.py --help
# auto-generated help showing: NAME  [required]

steps.label: Optional with default
Boolean flag
Run
steps.code:
python app.py process --verbose
python app.py greet Alice --greeting Hi

steps.label: Add commands
Run
steps.code:
python tool.py build myapp
python tool.py deploy --env staging
python tool.py --help  # lists both commands

tasks.title: Combine with Rich for beautiful output

steps.label: Use rich in commands
steps.code:
import typer
from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table

app = typer.Typer()
console = Console()

@app.command()
def list_packages():
    table = Table('Package', 'Version')
    table.add_row('requests', '2.32.3')
    table.add_row('numpy', '2.2.0')
    console.print(table)
